change_request: Add hreflang alternates to all generateMetadata pages — buildAlternates helper in lib/i18n.ts, alternates property in 5 page generateMetadata exports
acceptance_criteria: buildAlternates exported from lib/i18n.ts returns canonical and languages with en/es/x-default keys
all URLs use absolute base https://cofirsttimebuyergrants.com
x-default always points to English URL
homepage generateMetadata includes alternates with empty path
faq page generateMetadata includes alternates with /faq path
learn index generateMetadata includes alternates with /learn path
article detail generateMetadata includes alternates with resolved slug
county detail generateMetadata includes alternates with resolved county slug

scope.change_request: Add hreflang alternates to all generateMetadata pages — buildAlternates helper in lib/i18n.ts, alternates property in 5 page generateMetadata exports
acceptance_criteria: buildAlternates exported from lib/i18n.ts returns canonical and languages with en/es/x-default keys
all URLs use absolute base https://cofirsttimebuyergrants.com
x-default always points to English URL
homepage generateMetadata includes alternates with empty path
faq page generateMetadata includes alternates with /faq path
learn index generateMetadata includes alternates with /learn path
article detail generateMetadata includes alternates with resolved slug
county detail generateMetadata includes alternates with resolved county slug

llm_analysis.assessment: The change partially matches the request by adding hreflang alternates to the listed page metadata exports and introducing a shared helper in lib/i18n.ts, but the implementation appears incomplete and potentially incorrect for the acceptance criteria. The diff also includes many unrelated file changes, and the homepage metadata addition may not preserve existing metadata behavior.
concerns: Acceptance criteria requires buildAlternates to return canonical and languages with en/es/x-default keys, but the diff does not show the helper implementation details or canonical handling.
The homepage generateMetadata was added, but it is unclear whether it preserves existing title/description/JSON-LD values exactly as required.
The diff includes large unrelated changes to articles.json, site-canon.md, pipeline files, images, and review artifacts, which are out of scope for this request.
The article detail page is not listed among the changed source files, so the acceptance criterion for article detail alternates is not satisfied by the shown diff.

risks: app/[lang]/page.tsx:generateMetadata may override or omit pre-existing metadata fields, causing homepage SEO regressions or JSON-LD/metadata drift.
lib/i18n.ts:buildAlternates may generate incorrect absolute URLs or x-default/canonical mappings if path normalization is wrong, affecting all localized pages.
app/[lang]/learn/[slug]/page.tsx:generateMetadata may construct alternates from an unresolved or malformed slug, producing broken hreflang links for article detail pages.
app/[lang]/counties/[county]/page.tsx:generateMetadata may not match the resolved county slug format used by routing, leading to alternate URLs that do not resolve.
app/[lang]/faq/page.tsx and app/[lang]/learn/page.tsx: duplicated alternates wiring across pages increases the chance of inconsistent path handling or future drift.

llm_analysis.notes: The page metadata functions guard invalid lang values with notFound/empty metadata behavior, but the alternates helper edge-case behavior is not visible in the diff. There is no evidence of validation for empty paths, malformed slugs, or missing county/article records beyond existing page guards.

affected_apis: Next.js Metadata contract for generateMetadata in app/[lang]/page.tsx
Next.js Metadata contract for generateMetadata in app/[lang]/faq/page.tsx
Next.js Metadata contract for generateMetadata in app/[lang]/learn/page.tsx
Next.js Metadata contract for generateMetadata in app/[lang]/learn/[slug]/page.tsx
Next.js Metadata contract for generateMetadata in app/[lang]/counties/[county]/page.tsx
lib/i18n.ts exported helper API: buildAlternates(lang, path)
llm_analysis.notes: This is an internal metadata/helper change rather than a public API migration. However, any consumers of lib/i18n.ts will need to respect the new buildAlternates contract if they import it.

llm_analysis.recommendation: Add focused tests for buildAlternates output, absolute URL generation, x-default behavior, and metadata snapshots for the five pages to ensure title/description/JSON-LD remain unchanged.

rollback.head: eb0c14145e796f9e40621f54f79a620f41dd3836
rollback_steps: 2. Resolve any merge conflicts if prompted
rollback.additional_cleanup: Review affected files for any required data/config cleanup

overall.summary: The diff moves in the right direction by wiring alternates into several metadata exports, but it does not demonstrate that the helper satisfies the required canonical/language contract or that existing metadata remains unchanged. The presence of many unrelated file modifications and the lack of tests make this unsafe to approve.
key_concerns: Missing evidence that buildAlternates returns canonical plus en/es/x-default with the required absolute base URL.
No tests or snapshots to prove metadata parity for title/description/JSON-LD.
Unrelated large-file changes obscure the review and raise the risk of accidental regressions.

The presence of many unrelated file modifications and the lack of tests make this unsafe to approve.
